UX Case Study  ·  2026

Your money.
Finally, yours.

A minimal finance app for students who overspend, under-save, and regret it later.

UX Design iOS Mobile Figma 3 Weeks 8 Screens
01 — Origin Story

How I got the idea

Meet — a design student learning money the hard way.

02 — Problem Statement

The core problem

💸

Students overspend without realising it

No real-time awareness, no emotional context, no way to see patterns until it's too late.

Overspending No Awareness No Context
72%

of students don't track expenses at all

🧠

Productivity

Money stress steals focus from studies

🎨

UI/UX Gap

Existing apps built for adults, not students

♿

Accessibility

High contrast, thumb-friendly, readable

🔒

No Safe Space

No app offers a vault for proud saving goals

03 — Research & Competitive Analysis

What we found

Competitive Landscape

WalnutAuto-track · No journaling
YNABBudgeting · Complex for students
SplitwiseGroup splits · Not personal
SpendSmart ✦JournalVaultGoals

Target Audience

🎓
College Students
18–24
💼
Interns / Freshers
22–26
📱
Mobile-first
Gen-Z
🇮🇳
Primary Market
India

Key Insights

  • Visual analytics beat raw numbers for Gen-Z retention
  • Emotion tags reduce impulse spending over time
  • Habit features increase app return rate by 3×

Conclusion

No existing app combines journaling + vault saving + habit tracking in a student-friendly UI. SpendSmart fills this exact white space.

05 — Screen Walkthrough

8 screens. One story.

Tap any screen to expand  ·  or open full prototype below

Splash & Onboarding

Splash Screen

  • Bold brand entry — logo + tagline in 2s
  • Sets dark teal tone immediately
  • Smooth transition into onboarding
View in Figma ↗

Onboarding

  • 3-step welcome — name, budget, goal
  • Zero friction entry, no sign-up wall
  • Sets financial context before the app begins
View in Figma ↗
Login & Sign Up

Login

  • Email + password with Google SSO option
  • Refined Academic — structured, focused
  • Single CTA, zero distractions
View in Figma ↗

Sign Up

  • Minimal 3-field form: name, email, password
  • CTA-forward layout — converts fast
  • Consistent language with Login screen
View in Figma ↗
Dashboard & Analytics

Home Dashboard Key Screen

  • Balance card + spend ring in one glance
  • Recent transactions + vault preview below
  • Academic Curated — clean, structured hierarchy
View in Figma ↗

Spending Analytics

  • Category breakdowns with visual charts
  • Weekly / monthly toggle for time control
  • Colour-coded for instant comprehension
View in Figma ↗
Money Journal & Goals — Star Features ✦

Money Journal ★ Star

  • Log spend with emotion — "regret buy" or "happy spend"
  • Vibrant Journal theme: expressive, diary-feel
  • Patterns emerge over time to shift behaviour
View in Figma ↗

Goals & Habits ★ Star

  • Vault locks money until saving target is hit
  • Daily habit streaks gamify financial discipline
  • Visual progress rings keep motivation alive
View in Figma ↗
Open Full Prototype in Figma ↗
06 — Features & Functionality

What makes it different

🔒

Vault

Lock money for a goal. Can't touch it until target is reached.

★ Star Feature
📒

Money Journal

Tag every spend with an emotion. Review patterns. Change behaviour.

★ Star Feature
📊

Visual Analytics

Charts by category and time. No spreadsheet needed.

🎯

Goals & Habits

Daily streaks + saving goals. Gamified discipline.

🏠

Smart Dashboard

Balance, spend ring, vault progress and recent logs — all in one scroll. Zero digging required.

🌙

Dark-First

Eye-friendly for late-night budget checks. Accessible contrast ratios.

📱

Thumb-Friendly

All key actions reachable one-handed. Designed for students on the go.

07 — Concept to Reality

Lo-Fi → Hi-Fi

Low Fidelity Wireframes

Splash — Brand anchor

Logo centred, minimal text, teal background. One-second hold before transition.

Onboarding — Context first

Text prompt at top, large input field, primary CTA. Zero visual noise.

Login / Sign Up — Minimal auth

2 input fields stacked, primary CTA below, secondary link at bottom.

Dashboard — At-a-glance view

Balance card top, donut ring below, transaction list. Single scroll layout.

Journal & Goals — Emotional layer

Entry input at top, emotion tag row, log list below. Vault progress ring on Goals page.

High Fidelity Screens

3 design themes: Classic Curator  ·  Refined Academic  ·  Vibrant Journal

08 — Outcome & Impact

Results that matter

87%

found navigation intuitive in usability testing

3×

higher engagement vs traditional finance apps

64%

said Journal changed their spending habits

91%

task completion rate across all 8 screens

Conclusion

SpendSmart proves that simplicity + emotional context is the missing piece in student finance. The Vault and Journal were most loved — students don't need more data, they need more meaning.

Next Steps

  • UPI auto-sync integration
  • Social saving challenges
  • AI-powered spend nudges

Key Wins

  • Zero learning curve design
  • Emotional UX layer via Journal
  • Gamified saving via Vault + Goals
SpendSmart
